contaminant.name: Thermal Compound Deposits
contaminant.description: Thermal paste contamination arises from silicone or metal-based materials used in CPU cooling systems and it forms when paste spreads beyond interfaces during assembly or operation. This contaminant shows unique greasy texture and strong adhesion so it differs from dry dust or oxide layers by resisting simple wiping. On copper and aluminum surfaces, it hardens over time and removal challenges include residue buildup because paste bonds tightly. Laser cleaning works effectively since pulses vaporize paste without harming underlying nickel-plated areas.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Formaldehyde, Acetaldehyde - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Acetaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
